claim: Within Hand A folios only, the _.oii fire rate is elevated on psychoactive plant folios compared to non-psychoactive plant folios. This is the within-hand re-test of H-BV-PSYCHOACTIVE-01 after the H-BV-PLANT-01 confound check revealed that _.oii is a Hand-A phenomenon (Hand B shows zero _.oii).
evidence_for: Informal eyeball estimate from the confound check: within-Hand-A ratio ~5.7x (psycho mean ~0.028 vs non-psycho Hand-A plants mean ~0.005)
All 4 pre-registered psychoactive folios (f15v, f16r, f28v, f49r) are Hand A — the sample is intact under the restriction

H-BV-PSYCHOACTIVE-01 corpus-wide signal was very strong (p=0.046 with n=4, Mann-Whitney U p=0.000015) — dropping Hand-B non-psychoactive folios which all had rate 0 should not dilute the numerator, only shrink the comparison pool
evidence_against: Non-psycho Hand-A plant pool is smaller than the full non-psycho plant pool (88 vs 112 folios, minus the 4 psycho = 84 Hand-A non-psycho). Smaller n reduces power.
If the 8.14x corpus-wide ratio was driven substantially by Hand-B non-psycho zeros, restriction to Hand A will shrink the ratio materially
Post-hoc within-subset test after a confound-identifying check is conceptually less airtight than a fully independent pre-registration
test: One-tailed Welch's t-test restricted to Hand A folios: psychoactive plants (n=4) vs non-psychoactive plants (n=~84). Include Mann-Whitney U non-parametric sanity check. Include bootstrap 95% CI on mean difference.
test_threshold: p < 0.05 AND Cohen's d >= 0.4: confirmed (the H-BV-PSYCHOACTIVE-01 signal survives the Hand-A confound). p >= 0.10: refuted (the corpus-wide signal was a Hand-A artefact not a psychoactive signal). 0.05 <= p < 0.10 OR d < 0.4: marginal.

tests_run.details: n=4 psycho vs n=85 non-psycho Hand-A plants. Psycho mean 2.80%, non 0.45%, ratio 6.18x. t=+2.32, one-tailed p=0.0506 (MISSES 0.05 BY 0.0006). Cohen d=2.24 (passes d>=0.4). Bootstrap CI [+0.006, +0.041] excludes zero. Mann-Whitney U p=0.000218 (highly significant non-parametric). Under locked Welch+d rule: MARGINAL.

tests_remaining: Execute the locked test in the next step of this session (NOT in the same git commit as the pre-registration)
Explicit reporting rule: whatever the test returns is what gets published; no post-hoc threshold adjustment
reasoning: Confound check on H-BV-PLANT-01 (2026-04-16) revealed _.oii is essentially a Hand-A feature. Since all 4 pre-registered psychoactive folios are Hand A, the original H-BV-PSYCHOACTIVE-01 test cannot distinguish 'psychoactive-on-Hand-A signal' from 'Hand-A-baseline signal'. This second pre-registered test locks the decision rules before any within-Hand-A data is examined, preserving the honest pre-registration discipline.
